ocr.text: In the rapidly evolving semiconductor landscape, the capacity utilization of foundries continues to play a critical role in
addressing the burgeoning demand for automotive chips across the Asia-Pacific region. As illustrated in the accompanying
table, metrics such as Operating Margin and Net Profit Margin provide invaluable insights into the financial health of leading
foundries like TSMC. Notably, TSMC's Q1 2023 Operating Margin was reported at an impressive level, indicative of strong
operational efficiencies amid rising customer demand, quantified as Total.
Metric  Value  CapacityUtilization  FinancialRatios
OperatingMargin  NetProfitMargin  CurrentRatio  P/ERatio  DebttoEquity  ReturnonEquity
Furthermore, the company's Q3 2023 Return on Equity stands at 0.8, reflecting robust profitability. In terms of revenue
generation, the Total IP Revenue has experienced a substantial Year-on-Year Growth of 20%, driven in part by increased
Royalty Payments from Design Services.
AutomotiveChipSupplyStatus  Revenue(CNY)  NetIncome(CNY)  CAPEX(CNY)  GrossMargin(%)  MarketShare(%)  ProductionVolume(Units)  LeadTime(Weeks)

As we assess inventory strategies, it is pertinent to note that the Inventory Levels (CNY) reached 220M in Q1 2023, while the
Sales Forecast (CNY) for the upcoming quarter is projected at 100M.

This dynamic interplay between capacity, demand, and innovation underscores the critical nature of strategic investments,
particularly in R&D, as industry players navigate the complexities of supply chain constraints and evolving market needs.
